transcript.text: What's going on, Valentin?
Good afternoon.
Good, and you?
I'm doing pretty well.
Yeah, it's my third week at GrowthX, so, you know, every day I'm drinking from the fire hose, as they say, but it's a little bit more fun over here compared to other companies I've had.
Okay, nice if you enjoy it.
You're based in San Francisco, right?
I used to live there.
I live in Houston.
That's where I grew up.
What part of France are you in?
Well, we are based in Paris, but now I'm in Barcelona.
So, moving on this city right now.
Very cool.
Yeah, I studied abroad in Barcelona way back in the day.
Are you moving there or just spending some time?
I'm just spending some time because Hero has also an office in Barcelona, as we are spending our products in Spain too.
So, spending a couple of months here, and then let's see how it goes.
Not a bad life.
No, no, Great to meet you.
Appreciate you scheduling time for us to connect.

For today's conversation, Valentin, I really just want to get a better understanding of your interest in Growthx and make sure you walk away from today's conversation with a really good understanding of how we work and what you would get out of a partnership.
Does that sound good for our call today?
Yeah, yeah.
Maybe if you want, I can start by giving you some context about who we are, what we do, and what's the main point, the main issues we are facing with organic at the moment, organic traffic.
And then you can let me know how you could eventually help.
That sounds perfect.
Yeah, mean, doing a little bit of research, it looks like Hero is kind of like a square meets ramp is almost a good way to think about it.
I think it's a bit different in terms of audiences.

I think Ramp is addressing maybe bigger companies than what we are addressing, and so the acquisition is also maybe different, but I'm not a big, big expert of Ramp, but I saw the workshop of Marcel two days ago where he was explaining exactly the stuff you did with Ramp, and I was thinking that, yeah, maybe it was the company we should be able to compare with the, in terms of work we have to do in the organic, in the organic funnel, in the organic traffic.
Absolutely, yeah, sorry to interrupt.
You were going to give me kind of an overview of what's going on.
No, that's fine.
Feel free to interrupt if you want more information on some stuff.
But basically, Hero is a new bank, providing banking and financing solutions to small and medium businesses in Europe.
question.
you today.
Bye-bye.-bye.
We are now addressing four countries.
France is our main country.
Then we have Italy, Spain, and Germany.
Our acquisition funnel is quite classic for this kind of new banks.
You arrive on the website and you can open an account by yourself in 10 minutes.
Okay?
So the goal for us is to not ask to talk to you and leave you do the entire stuff by yourself.
We started to commercialize these products six, seven months ago.
And we started by scaling our paid channels because we needed to have a good traction at the beginning and have improved the concept.
lasting發現 were
that we had for these products.
So we started by paid channels, and now we have like a constant, how can I say it in English?
Sorry, I'm looking for the perfect word.
That's okay.
It doesn't have to be the perfect word either, but I appreciate you.
We have like a constant volume, like we are pretty happy with our paid channels.
Problem is, I think, because we focused a lot on this, we left for a while all the organic work that we could have done, etc.
So we've done some SEO, but I think some stuff, some pre-basic stuff, know, and not really ROI.
We think we think that's why don't have excellent ROI on this one.
You
Channels.
And so, yeah, what happened is just I saw the post of your CEO on LinkedIn, sounded quite interesting.
Checked on his latest workshop.

And yeah, so it could be interesting just to have a quick chat about it and see if you can help us, especially as I think we are a bit different from the other companies that you have because we are in Europe.
We are in different countries, so you have to adapt different strategies per country.
And so, yeah, that's that's pretty much it.
I don't know if it was clear.
Makes makes perfect sense.
Yeah.
Thanks for the overview, Valentin.
When it comes to like the organic traffic side of the house, who's creating content for you guys?
Are you working with an agency?
Do you have freelancers or creators in house?
Yeah, we have two freelancers?
Me

And what we did was basically, we had a roadmap with the keywords per cocoons, you know, I don't know if it's the same word in the U.S., but so we had like basically these categories of keywords that we need to write articles about.
And so the roadmap was almost for 12 months.
I think we had results that were quite okay.
But I have the feeling that with this new, like with all this AI search stuff that is going on, the strategy needs to be adapted to be able to, you know, to be on the AI results.
And so I'm just starting to deep dive this subject.
I have to be fully honest with you, not a big expertise on this area.
And so, and yeah, that's pretty much it.
Makes sense.
And I mean, nobody's an expert at this stage, right?
It's evolving every single day.
Even the people that say they're experts, they're still learning new things.
And I'm sure Marcel would agree.
Like he's spent the time to become an expert, but it's such a new field that, right, you're going to see different best practices on like a weekly basis.
How much, how much quantity, like, like how much content are you guys pumping out, would you say?
You have like a roadmap, right?
Like, are you guys doing, you know, one article a week, like a couple articles a week?
What does that look like?
I would say between two and three articles per week.
Two and three articles per week.
And is it just a matter of like scaling quantity or is there like a quality play here as well?
Like what's more important right now for you?
My, my, my main goal is that hero appears on, uh, child GPT and all the others.
When someone is looking for banking solutions in Europe, that's pretty much the goal.
So then the framework, the exact framework and how do we manage to do it, I leave it to you.
But this is what I'm trying to achieve and I want to have more organic needs coming on the funnel and not rely 100% on paid acquisition as we are doing today.
So it's about diversification.
It's about acquisition mix.
It's about these kinds of things.
Absolutely.
And would you say that like you're at a place in your search where you know you're going to go with a partner or are you thinking about like an agency partner, tools, hiring more?
How far along are you in your search, would you say?
Well, so now I think I'm doing two main things.
First thing is that I'm watching a lot of videos of people who probably deep dive a bit more than me on this subject, including they don't

Including Marcel, and second thing is also benchmarking solutions like yours, that I would love to hear more about, to understand what would be the perfect fit for us, in order to reach the goal that I just explained to you.
Perfect.
Let's jump in.
I'll give you like a quick overview of GrowthX and then we'll actually go like under the hood and I'll give you right, good context into how the partnership works.
So with GrowthX, you know, you can think of us as an end-to-end partner from strategy to content creation to even hitting publish in your CMS.
As you know, right, we're performing services guided by experts that we have in the loop and powered by our AI workflows.
So, you know, the reason that we think this is a important thing to invest in is what you saw in Marcel's workshop, the change from...
The traditional SEO to the AEO, GEO world, thinking about AI search, right?
Passing up traditional by 2028, this sort of zero click world that we're working in from a marketing perspective.
But also I think a really good statistic to keep in mind is that with less clicks, we're seeing higher intent with AI search visits.
There's like a study from SEMrush that shows that the AI search visits or visitors are 4.4 times more likely to convert and spend 3x as much time as a traditional search visit.

So, right, the name of the game is like how can you show up in these LLMs exactly as you mentioned, but also, you know, how are you going to be able to do that when there's such a longer tail of possible searches and queries, right, that someone could have.
And so, you know, what we found is the most important when it comes to showing up in a GEO lens is making sure the content is.
So,
We're researched and comprehensive, making sure it's up to date, right?
So with companies like RAMP, we do a lot of content repurposing and refreshing.
And we are creating content that is satisfying these criteria, and we're doing it at scale so that you give yourself more shots on goal.
But the real magic behind GrowthX is the strategy that we do up front, because our workflows would really be nothing without the right context.
That's powering them.
So we go super deep and create these artifacts around your company, the personas that you're trying to go after.
I'll show you a little bit more about what this looks like.
But, you know, high level GrowthX, like we're doing this through Marcel's expertise, and he has been a marketing leader at every company that he's worked at, right?
From IBM, creating securityintelligence.com that drove over 50 million in pipeline, 17 million in revenue for the business.
All the
the way to being Scale AI's first CMO, right, through their acquisition, and then the story with DeepGram that you heard him talk about in the workshop.
Any just questions at a high level so far before I dive deeper and deeper?
No, no, it's pretty clear.
Perfect.
So with GrowthX, right, it's, well, you could work with a traditional agency that's maybe only going to create, right, briefs for you, but not do any of the content creation.

You could try to learn how to create these workflows across all of these different tools, or you could work with us, and then, right, we would basically manage the whole process without putting more bandwidth on your team.
So that's really what you get with GrowthX is we get to daily publishing, and we've got two phases of our partnership.
The first is an eight-week strategy sprint, and we'll actually do publishing during those eight weeks.
The second would be a 12-month growth to execution, and you don't have to, right, like, make a commitment.
And to that 12 months until you get to the end of the strategy sprint.
So that's how you would partner with us is we'd start on the strategy side and then move into that 12 month content creation centered phase.
What questions do you have about like how our partnership works?
The first question that I have in mind now is that on our case, we have to, of course, write the content in French, German, Spanish and Italian.
Sorry.
Is it something that you manage on your side?
Do you, how do you work with this?
Is this okay that already occurred?
Because on our side, we know that translation is not enough.
You need to have a native person watching at the article most of the time and correcting the.
The translators, even if you use the best one on the market.
Yeah, I mean, it makes a lot of sense.

What we would do there, I'd need to check internally in terms of the extent of our capabilities, but we've got folks working in like 20 plus different countries for growthx, so we likely have like fluency in-house.
Some clients, you know, we have someone basically look at the drafts and, right, like make edits themselves because they're more fluid in the language than anyone we have in-house.
But it is something that I would need to check on to make sure that like Marcel would want to bring on a client that needs support across, right, all four of those languages.
Just to confirm, do you have folks in-house that can speak all those different languages and potentially review the articles that we create?

Yeah, we have, and we also have a translator that we are paying on our side for this kind of stuff, but just wanted to know how, I mean, there are several possibilities on this one, just wanted to know if this is something that you already tweeted on the past with other clients, but yeah, we'll find out.
Absolutely, it's a great question, and I'll get more specifics from Marcel for you.

That translator, could they be someone that, you know, hypothetically is looking at each article that we draft and making changes to it, or are they more on like a per hour basis with your company and you'd want to be cautious there?
Well, we are both, I mean, we, we are quite, I mean, what's the exact question you have on this side?
Because we have, we have, have sales on in-house that are from every country.
Okay.
basically, these are the people that we ask for help when we need, but this is not people that we can use on a daily basis.
to do this kind of work, because obviously this is not their main work, and in that case, when we have a lot of volume, we go on freelance on the freelance site.
Okay, got it.
I guess, what would you need from GrowthX in terms of how much we could verify across all four languages?
I just want to understand what you would need from us there.

But, well, if you can do it on your site, of course, I'm interested in knowing and for what price, so I can benchmark on my site, and know if it's worth for me to leave it to you, or if I want to have it in-house.
Makes sense.
Makes sense.
Sounds good there.

And to show you some specific examples and how they show up in search, I want to start with these guides that we created for augment code, so all these different guides that you see here were created by us, and this is a really good example.
Because augment code, they're going up against Cursor, GitHub, very popular tools in the no-code space, or in the AI coding space.
And this is a great example of seeing kind of what we can do when it comes to content creation.
You know, this table was created by us, everything that you're seeing in this article.
And if I went into Google, and I just searched AI coding tools for enterprises, you'll see that augment is the first article that shows up on the AI overview.
You can see us in the description.
You know, if you scroll down, well, I guess you don't see us on the first page there.
But the idea here is that, right, you're getting quick results using GrowthX.
And the point is that, you know, we're very strategically like going after the keywords that you want to target, right?
We're going after these topics that...
That are relevant to you from either a use case perspective or a competitive perspective.
And that's kind of the big thing with GrowthX is we're not just creating content without any strategy behind it.
We're creating content that's aligned to what the ICP, the personas you care about, would be thinking about when it comes to conversations with ChatGPT.
It's basically like this approach that's almost inception, right?
Like what are the questions that they might have?
How do we create content that best answers those questions?
Okay.
Some more examples here, just beyond like editorial, we created this per diem calculator for Ramp.
So you could go in and there's a page for, right, any city.
Let's say like just looking at what's the daily cost in Dallas.
This is more of like a program.
This
Play that we created for RAMP.
We also created this expense classifier.
So, you know, let's say someone searches for the expense category around office furniture.
If I do the same search in perplexity, RAMP is the first article that comes up there.
So, you know, the outcomes that we're driving for clients, Valentin, it's really around organic traffic.
If you look at RAMP, who we've worked with for over a year, when they got to GrowthX, right, there may be around 250,000, 300,000 organic traffic.
We've gotten them, you know, over 50% year over year from where they were before.

If you take an example on the opposite end of the spectrum, Tiro, who had pretty much no domain authority when they got to us, we were able to, right, drive their traffic 10 to 20x to get to a place where they're starting to see a lot more consistent traffic hitting their website.
And so, you know, those are...
The output, the output and outcomes, examples and outcomes we deliver for clients.
I want to give you more context on how it all works, right?
So as I mentioned, the strategy sprint, we're going to be creating these artifacts that really allow us to make the workflows as effective as possible.
So company context, this is one we did for Engine that's in more of the travel booking space.
You know, we're using this through expert interviews with your team.
We're doing this by ingesting call recordings or any other resources that you might have.
And we're going to do this, you know, for the company context, but also across all your personas.
So for Engine, we created these personas across different industries.
Like construction was one that they wanted to go after.
And we've got all these different personas that show up in terms of like, what are their fears?
What motivates them?
How do you win their trust?
What do they care about?
And so you can see, right, when we create these artifacts up front, we can create content that's going to be much more effective because it factors all of this into what we're creating.
And then once we've got that right, we're going to calibrate this right to your tone, to your branding, then we can go super fast.
So if you take that engine example, you know, we've published 37 articles for them to date.
They're in, you know, week seven of their strategy sprint.
In that eight-week period, you're not just getting strategy.
You're not just getting these artifacts.
Like, we're actually going out and creating content at scale.
I'm going to go through that augment code example so that you can see a little bit more about the workflows themselves.
But I guess, like, how is this, like, resonating?
Is this helping you better understand, like, what GrowthX would do for the business?
Anything, like, that you're confused about?
No, no.
This is what you are showing.
Clusters, it's what we are already doing, so we have this exact same strategy of doing categories and writing articles based on keywords and difficulty in the rank.
So for now, nothing really different from what we are doing, but I would love to hear more about your workflows and stuff, how you create the content at scale, as you explained.
Definitely, and it sounds like the main problem is just that you can't create the scale that you want to.
Like, you have this process figured out, but you can't figure out how to, like, bring AI into the equation so that you can do more with less.
Is that, like, the heart?
Yeah, yeah, I mean, we are, I think, if, I think we can, we could have better framework, you know, it's, for me, this kind of stuff is all about framework.
What is the best framework to do it, and we just, you know, repeat it, repeat it, repeat it again.
I think our framework is...
It's not perfect at the moment, and that's what we are looking for.
And so I think this is the piece, right?
It's probably like the most powerful addition to your existing framework, and it's the AI workflows.
So take the augment code example.
There's a bunch of different workflows we have here.
One's going to create an article with an image.
One's more of a comparison to different tools.
Another, right, is a refresh.
And so if I go into the article generation pipeline, these are different articles that we've published on their site.
And each of these steps is a workflow with several steps inside of, you know, that piece here.
And so what I'm going to do is I'm going to deconstruct this for you so that you can see under the hood.
If I take that article, right, that I showed you earlier, you'll see that what's happening is we are creating the assignment brief as the first step.
So we're actually creating the brief and the assignment.
And then using the same right tooling to go out and draft an outline, fact check, do cross-linking, add SEO meta tags, and then publish the content to your website.
So this is the whole process and you'll see the outputs as I go along.
What's cool here is since this is all, you know, like a code powered engine, I can inspect any of these different workflows so that you can really see like what's happening behind the scenes.
So the first step, the assignment brief, what we're doing is we're going in and we're fetching keyword traffic, the top organic search results.
We're looking at SERP ranking factors and competitor content, and then we're actually delivering this brief, which is, you know, super robust, right?
You get this brief, you're ready to go.
Another step in here is we're going to do some deep research.
So for us to do effective deep research, what we need to do is generate research questions.
And these questions, right, we want our content.
It performed as the content's created, but in reality, it's hundreds of different steps informed by, right, all of these different APIs that we're leveraging across SEMrush or other sources.

And then it's actually validating, you know, your article against trusted sources that are out there so that at the end of the day, when we hit publish, it's not just like AI generated content with no voice behind it.
It's AI generated content that's powered by the company context, the persona context, and then the hundreds of steps that are performed in between, all with an expert from GrowthX in the loop.
So, you know, on the GrowthX side, our chief content officer, he was the editor-in-chief at TechCrunch.
On the client delivery side, we've got, you know, director who is the head of SEO at TripAdvisor and Fanatics.
On the managing editor side.
We've got someone who built the G2 website.
So what you get from growthx is you get a managing director, a managing editor, and a content manager.
And all the while that we are creating content, you know, they're using the software to be able to actually go in and make changes to the draft, right?
So they can actually make changes in the loop as opposed to just leveraging the AI workflows and that's it.
But, you know, I'm getting better and better at articulating this succinctly.
So thanks for bearing with me today.
I know I wasn't too succinct.
But hopefully this kind of shows you what you'd get out of the growthx partnership.
And, you know, there's a lot more showing, right, than telling in terms of, like, what our technology is actually doing and what we're capable of.
What's your thoughts on next steps here, Valentin?
Is the strategy sprint something you want to take a closer look at?
Well, have to go in two minutes.
I have a hard stop.
So I won't be able to.
To last on this call, but maybe if you can just send me a recap by email and also the growthx not taker of this call because sorry I did not register on my side.
So I can take a deeper look at it and maybe we can talk again if I have questions on the next day, if that's fine for you.
That sounds great.
Yeah, you would be on the low end of our strategy sprint pricing.
So it'd be a 20K fee.
And again, we would do publishing right during those eight weeks for you guys.
One thing to just keep in mind is we're typically like four to six weeks out just based on capacity.
There's like a lot of demand for our services right now.
So we do need to be a bit more planning ahead when it comes to your kickoff date.
So if you're interested, right, just keep us in the loop.
And if we need to do a deep dive with Marcel or our VP of customer.
That would be like the next step that we we'd be happy to take.
So keep us posted there and we'll go from there.
Okay.
Perfect.
Thank you very much.
Thanks Valentin.
Nice to meet you.
See you later.
Nice.
Bye bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Explore GrowthX's AI-powered content creation services for Hero's organic traffic growth across European markets.](https://fathom.video/share/iHMWb2czaq8rawhz5qdk3_saCx9Ds3Tg?tab=summary&timestamp=70.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [Hero seeks to diversify acquisition mix, boost organic traffic, and appear in AI search results for European banking solutions](https://fathom.video/share/iHMWb2czaq8rawhz5qdk3_saCx9Ds3Tg?tab=summary&timestamp=87.0)
  - [GrowthX offers end-to-end AI-powered content strategy and creation, with expert oversight and multilingual capabilities](https://fathom.video/share/iHMWb2czaq8rawhz5qdk3_saCx9Ds3Tg?tab=summary&timestamp=561.0)
  - [Hero currently produces 2-3 articles/week; GrowthX proposes 8-week strategy sprint ($20K) + optional 12-month execution phase](https://fathom.video/share/iHMWb2czaq8rawhz5qdk3_saCx9Ds3Tg?tab=summary&timestamp=768.0)

## Topics

### Hero's Current Situation

  - [New bank for SMBs in Europe (France, Italy, Spain, Germany)](https://fathom.video/share/iHMWb2czaq8rawhz5qdk3_saCx9Ds3Tg?tab=summary&timestamp=169.0)
  - [10-min self-service account opening](https://fathom.video/share/iHMWb2czaq8rawhz5qdk3_saCx9Ds3Tg?tab=summary&timestamp=199.0)
  - [Scaled paid channels successfully; organic efforts basic/low ROI](https://fathom.video/share/iHMWb2czaq8rawhz5qdk3_saCx9Ds3Tg?tab=summary&timestamp=225.0)
  - [2 freelancers creating content (2-3 articles/week)](https://fathom.video/share/iHMWb2czaq8rawhz5qdk3_saCx9Ds3Tg?tab=summary&timestamp=357.0)
  - [Using keyword clusters, but seeking improved framework](https://fathom.video/share/iHMWb2czaq8rawhz5qdk3_saCx9Ds3Tg?tab=summary&timestamp=361.0)

### GrowthX's Offering

  - [End-to-end partner: strategy → content creation → CMS publishing](https://fathom.video/share/iHMWb2czaq8rawhz5qdk3_saCx9Ds3Tg?tab=summary&timestamp=561.0)
  - [AI-powered workflows guided by industry experts](https://fathom.video/share/iHMWb2czaq8rawhz5qdk3_saCx9Ds3Tg?tab=summary&timestamp=580.0)
  - [8-week strategy sprint ($20K) + optional 12-month execution](https://fathom.video/share/iHMWb2czaq8rawhz5qdk3_saCx9Ds3Tg?tab=summary&timestamp=768.0)
  - [Creates company/persona context artifacts to inform content](https://fathom.video/share/iHMWb2czaq8rawhz5qdk3_saCx9Ds3Tg?tab=summary&timestamp=691.0)
  - [Drives significant organic traffic growth (e.g., Ramp: 50%+ YoY, Tiro: 10-20x)](https://fathom.video/share/iHMWb2czaq8rawhz5qdk3_saCx9Ds3Tg?tab=summary&timestamp=1174.0)

### GrowthX's Process

  - [Comprehensive strategy artifacts (company context, personas)](https://fathom.video/share/iHMWb2czaq8rawhz5qdk3_saCx9Ds3Tg?tab=summary&timestamp=1222.0)
  - [AI workflows for brief creation, research, drafting, fact-checking](https://fathom.video/share/iHMWb2czaq8rawhz5qdk3_saCx9Ds3Tg?tab=summary&timestamp=1437.0)
  - [Expert-in-the-loop for quality control and edits](https://fathom.video/share/iHMWb2czaq8rawhz5qdk3_saCx9Ds3Tg?tab=summary&timestamp=1588.0)
  - [Calibration to brand voice and tone](https://fathom.video/share/iHMWb2czaq8rawhz5qdk3_saCx9Ds3Tg?tab=summary&timestamp=1629.0)
  - [Ability to create content at scale (e.g., 37 articles in 7 weeks for Engine)](https://fathom.video/share/iHMWb2czaq8rawhz5qdk3_saCx9Ds3Tg?tab=summary&timestamp=1285.0)

### Multilingual Considerations

  - [Hero needs content in French, German, Spanish, Italian](https://fathom.video/share/iHMWb2czaq8rawhz5qdk3_saCx9Ds3Tg?tab=summary&timestamp=820.0)
  - [GrowthX has global team; exact capabilities TBC](https://fathom.video/share/iHMWb2czaq8rawhz5qdk3_saCx9Ds3Tg?tab=summary&timestamp=851.0)
  - [Potential for collaboration with Hero's in-house language experts](https://fathom.video/share/iHMWb2czaq8rawhz5qdk3_saCx9Ds3Tg?tab=summary&timestamp=901.0)

### AI Search Optimization

  - [Focus on comprehensive, up-to-date content](https://fathom.video/share/iHMWb2czaq8rawhz5qdk3_saCx9Ds3Tg?tab=summary&timestamp=600.0)
  - [Targeting longer-tail queries and persona-specific questions](https://fathom.video/share/iHMWb2czaq8rawhz5qdk3_saCx9Ds3Tg?tab=summary&timestamp=635.0)
  - [Examples: Augment Code ranking for "AI coding tools", Ramp's interactive tools](https://fathom.video/share/iHMWb2czaq8rawhz5qdk3_saCx9Ds3Tg?tab=summary&timestamp=1042.0)

## Next Steps

  - [GrowthX to send meeting recap and AI-generated notes](https://fathom.video/share/iHMWb2czaq8rawhz5qdk3_saCx9Ds3Tg?tab=summary&timestamp=1680.0)
  - [Valentin to review info and consider strategy sprint option](https://fathom.video/share/iHMWb2czaq8rawhz5qdk3_saCx9Ds3Tg?tab=summary&timestamp=1675.0)
  - [Potential deep-dive with Marcel (GrowthX CEO) or VP of Customer Success](https://fathom.video/share/iHMWb2czaq8rawhz5qdk3_saCx9Ds3Tg?tab=summary&timestamp=1706.0)
  - [Hero to keep GrowthX informed of interest (4-6 week lead time for kickoff)](https://fathom.video/share/iHMWb2czaq8rawhz5qdk3_saCx9Ds3Tg?tab=summary&timestamp=1718.0)

action_items.description: Email Valentin recap of meeting + GrowthX note-taker notes. Include strategy sprint pricing ($20K), 4-6 week lead time for kickoff.
